title: Analyst Suggestions
description: Per-pair informational sidecar capturing off-scope signals that might be interesting for follow-up work (per ECON-AS). Governs `results/{pair_id}/analyst_suggestions.json`. Any agent (dana / evan / vera / ray / ace / quincy) may file an entry during pair work. Entries are informational only — NO lifecycle fields, NO automated trigger, NO workflow state. Rendered on the Methodology page as a read-only table under 'Analyst Suggestions for Future Work'. Per META-CF (Contract File Standard) — producer validates before save; Ace (consumer) validates before render via `scripts/validate_schema.py`. Schema ownership is Evan (per META-CF schema-owner discipline); per-entry `proposed_by` can be any agent code, not just evan.

pair_id.description: Canonical pair identifier (snake_case, e.g. `hy_ig_v2_spy`). Matches the `results/{pair_id}/` directory name.

schema_version.description: Semver of this instance. Bumped per META-CF when entries are appended or edited.

last_updated_at.description: ISO 8601 date-time of the most recent edit to this instance.

suggestions.description: List of analyst suggestions. Order is insertion order (append-only from the agent's perspective). Empty array is valid — consumers render no section when the array is empty.

notes.description: Freeform top-level notes (e.g., retro-fit context, wave reference).

suggestion_entry.description: One informational suggestion. All eight fields are mandatory. No lifecycle or workflow fields (no `status`, no `assigned_to`, no `priority`) — suggestions are captured metadata, not tickets.

signal_name.description: Human-readable signal label (e.g. `NFCI (Chicago Fed National Financial Conditions Index)`, `Yield Curve 10Y-3M`).

proposed_by.description: Agent code of the proposer. Any producer or QA agent may propose; there is no restriction to Evan.

source.description: Upstream source — `FRED`, `BAML`, `Yahoo`, `constructed`, `OECD`, etc. Free-text but canonical vocabulary encouraged for grep-auditability.

observation.description: The metric observed that prompted the suggestion. Example: `correlation 0.87 with SPY 21d forward return; Granger p=0.01 at lag 2`.

rationale.description: Plain-English one- to three-sentence rationale for why this observation is noteworthy. META-ELI5 compliant.

possible_use_case.description: Free-text tag describing the suggested follow-up path. Canonical vocabulary (not enforced by enum, but encouraged): `variant family`, `new pair`, `regime overlay`, `cross-pair comparison`. Multiple tags allowed as free-text.

caveats.description: Honest caveats — small sample size, overfitting risk, look-ahead bias suspicion, stationarity concerns, data-quality flags. An empty caveats field is a META-ELI5 red flag; at minimum state 'none observed at time of filing'.

date_filed.description: ISO 8601 date the suggestion was filed (YYYY-MM-DD).

notes.description: Optional freeform notes — references to specific exploratory artifacts, related suggestions, etc.
